uk: Неділя 5-та по Зісланні Святого Духа. Глас 4. Єв. 5.
🕃 Перенесення мощів святих безсрібників Кира і Івана
saints.en: 4th Sunday after Pentecost. Translation of the Relics of Sts. Unmercenaries Cyrus & John

rubrics:
Вечірня: «Блажен муж». На «Господи, взиваю» - 10 стихир: 4 воскресних і 6 безср., Сл.: безср., І нині: богородичний-догмат 4-го гласу. Вхід. Прокімен дня. Стиховня воскресна. По «Отче наш» – тропар воскресний, Сл.: безср., І нині: богород. воскресний за гласом «Слава». Відпуст великий воскресний, з помин. безср.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь» тропар воскресний (2), Сл.: безср., І нині: богород. воскресний за гласом «Слава». Катизми чергові, сідал. воскресні. «Ангельський собор», іпакой, степенна та прокімен 4-го гласу. Єв. – Лк. 113 зач.; 24, 12-35. «Воскресіння Христове». Пс. 50. Стихира недільна. Канон воскресний, хрестовоскресний, Богородиці і безср. Катавасія: «Відкрию уста мої». По 3-ій пісні – кондак і сідальний безср. По 6-ій – кондак-ікос воскресний. На 9-ій пісні – «Чеснішу від херувимів». По 9-ій пісні – «Свят Господь Бог наш» і світильний воскресний, Сл.: безср., І нині: богород. воскресного світильного. На Хвалитних – 8 стихир воскресних, Сл.: стихира євангельська, І нині: «Преблагословенна». По вел. славослов'ї – тропар «Воскресши з гробу». Відпуст великий воскресний, з помин. безср.
Часи: на всіх – тропар воскресний, Слава: на 3-му і 9-му – тропар безср., на 6-му – храму. Кондаки: на 1-му – воскресний, на 3-му і 9-му – безср., на 6-му храму.
Літургія: антифони: два перших – воскресні, а третій – звичайний з воскресним приспівом, а також і «Прийдіте, поклонімся». Тропар воскресний, Сл.: кондак воскресний, І нині: богород. воскрес. за гласом «Слава». Прокімен воскресний. Ап. – Рим. 103 зач.; 10, 1-10. Єв. – Мт. 27 зач.; 8, 28 – 9, 1. Причасний «Хваліте».

troparion_en.text: O Christ God, / You have given us the miracles of Your martyrs, Cyrus and John, / as an invincible rampart; / through their prayers, frustrate the plans of the heathens, / and strengthen the faith of the Orthodox Christians, / for You alone are good and love mankind.

kontakion_en.text:
(Podoben: “Today the Virgin...”)
Having received the gift of miracles through divine grace, O saints, / you work wonders in the world unceasingly. / You remove all of our passions through your invisible surgery, / divinely-wise Cyrus and glorious John, / for you are truly divine physicians.

matched_oca_saint: Translation of the Relics of the holy and wonderworking Unmercenaries Cyrus and John

oca_saints.name: Translation of the Relics of the holy and wonderworking Unmercenaries Cyrus and John

troparion.text: O Christ God, / You have given us the miracles of Your martyrs, Cyrus and John, / as an invincible rampart; / through their prayers, frustrate the plans of the heathens, / and strengthen the faith of the Orthodox Christians, / for You alone are good and love mankind.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “Today the Virgin...”)
Having received the gift of miracles through divine grace, O saints, / you work wonders in the world unceasingly. / You remove all of our passions through your invisible surgery, / divinely-wise Cyrus and glorious John, / for you are truly divine physicians.

oca_saints.name: Venerable Xenophon, Abbot of Robeika, Novgorod

troparion.text: You appeared as a splendid growth in the wilderness, Venerable Father,  / for from your youth you chose a life of purity, following your spiritual instructor,  / and by his admonitions, you turned your mind to heavenly things.  / You dwelt in the wilderness and there you founded a monastery, becoming a wise counselor for your flock,  / shining as a bright lamp in Great Novgorod with honor and strength.  / Therefore, Christ enriched you with miracles and glorified you.  / O Xenophon our Father, pray to Christ God for the salvation of our souls.

oca_saints.name: Venerable Sergius and Herman, Wonderworkers of Valaam

troparion.text: Fulfilling the Gospel of Christ and loving him alone, / despising earthly cares you settled on an island. / There you fought against the invisible foe, / subjecting your bodies to the Spirit by fasting, vigil and prayer. / Crowned with glory and now standing before the Trinity, / pray, O Fathers Sergius and Herman, / that our souls may be saved!

kontakion.text: You left the world to follow in Christ’s footsteps, / reaching Lake Neva and the island of Valaam. / There you lived the angelic life / and were translated to the heavenly bridal chamber. / As you stand with the angels remember the children whom you gathered, / that we may joyfully cry to you: / Rejoice, blessed Fathers Sergius and Herman!”

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Paul, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

oca_saints.name: Icon of the Mother of God “Of the Three Hands”

troparion.text:
(Podoben: “Go quickly before...”)
From Palestine, the godly-minded Savva brought your divine Icon to us,  / O holy Bride of God, known as the Icon “Of the Three Hands."  / Wherefore, since the Monastery of Hilandar has acquired it as their boast,  / they hymn you, O Theotokos, crying out in exultation:  / Hail, full of grace, the Lord is with you.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “O victorious Leader...”)
Come, let us venerate the most revered Icon of "Of the Three Hands” with faith and piety.  / O Maiden, preserve your Monastery from all misfortunes and adversities,  / and also those who cry to you; "Hail, defender of all."

troparion.text: By sharing in the ways of the Apostles, / you became a successor to their throne. / Through the practice of virtue, you found the way to divine contemplation, O inspired one of God; / by teaching the word of truth without error, you defended the Faith, / even to the shedding of your blood. / Hieromartyr Basil, entreat Christ God to save our souls.

Your holy martyr Pappias, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Pappias;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

troparion.text: In you, O Father, was preserved with exactness what was according to the image;  /  for you took up the Cross and followed Christ.  /  By so doing, you taught us to disregard the flesh, for it passes away;  /  but to care for the soul, since it is immortal.  /  Therefore, O Venerable Sergius, your spirit rejoices with the Angels.

kontakion.text: Finding delight in abstinence, you subdued the desires of the flesh. /   Revealed as one nurtured in the Faith,  /  you blossomed like the Tree of Life in Paradise, /   O Holy Father Sergius.
